x-enumeration-literals.name: Functional Requirement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines what the product/service must do.

Comment:
It describes features, behaviors, and business functions.

Example: The system must allow customers to transfer funds between accounts.
Focus: Capabilities and actions

x-enumeration-literals.name: Technical Requirement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines how the system is built or implemented technically.

Comment:
Includes architecture, technology stack, performance, integration, and security design.

Example: The system must support REST APIs and encrypt data using AES-256.
Focus: Technology and system design

x-enumeration-literals.name: Operation Requirement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines how the product/service is operated in daily use.

Comment:
Includes processes, monitoring, support, and availability.

Example: The system must operate 24/7 with 99.9% uptime and support batch processing at night.
Focus: Day-to-day operations

x-enumeration-literals.name: Legal Requirement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines laws, regulations, and compliance rules that must be followed.

Comment:
Includes regulatory banking laws, data protection (e.g., GDPR), AML/KYC rules.

Example: Customer identity must be verified before account opening (KYC compliance).
Focus: Compliance and law

x-enumeration-literals.name: Testing Requirement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines what must be validated to ensure quality and correctness.

Comment:
Includes test cases, acceptance criteria, and quality checks.

Example: All payment transactions must pass functional, security, and performance testing before release.
Focus: Verification and validation

x-enumeration-literals.name: Sales and Marketing Requirement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines how the product is positioned, sold, and promoted.

Comment:
Includes target customers, pricing strategy, channels, and messaging.

Example: The product must support promotional interest rates for new customers for 6 months.
Focus: Market adoption and revenue generation

x-enumeration-literals.name: Servicing Requirement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines how the product is supported after delivery to customers.

Comment:
Includes customer support, issue handling, maintenance, and updates.

Example: Customer service must resolve account issues within 24 hours.
Focus: Post-sale support

x-enumeration-literals.name: Production Requirement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines how the product/service is built or delivered into production environment.

Comment:
Includes deployment, manufacturing, release process, and scalability.

Example: All releases must go through staging before production deployment.
Focus: Build and release lifecycle

x-enumeration-literals.name: Suitability Requirement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Defines whether the product is appropriate for its intended purpose or customer segment.

Comment:
Ensures product fits customer needs and use cases.

Example: The loan product must be suitable for small business owners with irregular income.
Focus: Fit-for-purpose
